title: Treasury and Artificial Intelligence
summary: This is a Treasury hub page serving as a repository for information on Treasury's use of AI and AI policy in the financial sector. It aggregates links to FinCEN alerts, AI use case inventories, AI strategy documents, and related executive orders and OMB memos. The page does not contain substantive research findings or direct market/macro relevance.
key_points: Page is a navigation/repository hub linking to Treasury AI resources, not a standalone research document
References FinCEN alert on deepfake fraud schemes (November 2024) as a linked resource
Lists Treasury AI Use Case Inventory (January 2026) and AI Compliance Plan
References executive orders EO 14179, EO 13960, and OMB memos M-25-22 and M-25-21 on AI governance
No substantive policy announcements, economic data, or market-moving content on the page itself
Published_at not stated in source metadata
why_it_matters: This source does not contain directly applicable content for market structure or macro catalysts analysis. It is a static information hub aggregating links to AI policy documents. The FinCEN deepfake alert and AI governance materials may have indirect regulatory implications for financial institutions but the page itself provides no actionable research findings.
notes: Source is a Treasury AI hub page containing only links to documents, not the documents themselves. Not relevant to market structure or macro catalysts research areas. The FinCEN deepfake fraud alert and AI strategy documents linked here may warrant separate capture if they contain substantive regulatory content. Page type is listing_page per CORE RULE 6.

reasoning: Navigation/repository page with no substantive content
Contains only links to external documents, no original research findings
No economic data, policy decisions, or market-moving content
Not relevant to market structure or macro catalysts topics
